content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN integers.

Count the number of **ordered pairs** (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that:


1‚â§i,j‚â§N1 \leq i, j \leq N1‚â§i,j‚â§N, and
Aij‚â§AjA_i^j \leq A_jAij‚Äã‚â§Aj‚Äã

That is, AiA_iAi‚Äã raised to the power jjj doesn't exceed AjA_jAj‚Äã.
Note that we're counting ordered pairs, meaning the pair (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) is different from the pair (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of elements in the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of ordered pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) that satisfy the given condition.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
